comment.body:
If you see the mirror URL domain paymoney.click, I have blocked the mirror replacement injection that occurs on the client side:

> The WordPress plugin pro-seo-booster injects an inline script that fires on `DOMContentLoaded`, iterates all links, and wraps their href values through paymoney.click using `btoa`.

If a different URL appears, I will check it again.

comment.body:
Ah, I checked another Telegram channel redirects from turkzzersifsa1.blog, turkzzersifsa2.blog:

```adblock
##.entry-content a[href=https://t.me/xlifevip]
##.entry-content a[href*="dC5tZS94bGlmZXZpcA=="]
```

Ultimately, how about applying the rules for expressing the links in the main content? Regardless of the domain.

```adblock
##.entry-content a[href="https://t.me/turkifsalifevip"]
##.entry-content a[href*="dC5tZS90dXJraWZzYWxpZmV2aXA"]

##.entry-content a[href=https://t.me/xlifevip]
##.entry-content a[href*="dC5tZS94bGlmZXZpcA=="]
```

comment.body:
Your rule works. It seems that they now serve ads for almost every prompt related to development
Ad disclaimer: The ad itself is hidden by chatgpt.com##div[class]:has(> div.flex > button[aria-label="Ad options"]) A disclaimer element appears shortly after the advertisement, and remains visible:
Source: `
`
`chatgpt.com##.border-token-border-default.mt-1.border-t.py-3.text-sm` or `chatgpt.com##div[class]:has(> button > span > svg[data-rtl-flip])` work for the disclaimer. They change the text sometimes: "Your conversations remain private" or similar (they'll probably also translate it to the user language).

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Din.amazon.mShop.android.shopping)

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.amazon.mShop.android.shopping](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Din.amazon.mShop.android.shopping)

### Comment
> 🔹If i set dns service to dns.adguard-dns.com or family.adguard-dns.com or unfiltered server in device settings i can browse all apps except amazon (india) app normally. Even amazon website (.in/.com) loads properly in browser. Amazon app shows an unending loading screen as shown in attached screenshots.
> 🔹I also noticed some difficulty occasionally while downloading ad-ons in Firefox browser.
> 🔹 I tried device restart etc. but it does not fix this issue.
> 🔹 Even dns check shows server is connected (singapore).
> 🔹 Using alternate services like quad9 or controlD works fine. Even with private dns turned off, this issue does not happen.
> 🔹 This issue has been happening from past 4 days.



### Screenshots

  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

comment.body:
I have made a 3½ min dedicated video of the issue which can be accessed at: https://jumpshare.com/share/H7eGePsQm8sPMg5jrxDn

I could have tried to test family and default servers too but that would only make it too long.

Note that i am using using a Prepaid Airtel India sim and this issue is for past 5 days. And i also restarted & restored my device yesterday but the issue is still there.

 I tried other alternatives such as p2.freedns.controld.com or toggling the private dns altogether off and it works fine using them; only all 3 adguard dns options are giving me this problem. Thanks for your support.

comment.body: @zloyden for many of these sites there are at least two domains. one that ends with `.com` and the other `.tube`, so it's best to either add `manysex.*` or `manysex.com,manysex.tube`.
